title: cistus × incanus subsp. corsicus
intro: Cistus × incanus subsp. corsicus, also known as Corsican rockrose, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Cistus genus. It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. Cistus × incanus subsp. corsicus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are used to soothe inflammation, calm the nervous system, and promote overall well-being.
